note: Soft tier classification for detected REVIEW model IDs. Live detection is truth; this table only pre-tags KNOWN ids so cad-config can auto-place them. Staleness is soft: an unmatched id gets tier=null and the user places it manually - it never errors. `exclude` drops non-text modalities (embeddings, audio, image, etc.) that cannot do structured text review, so they never appear as candidates. Rules are ordered most-specific-first; match is case-insensitive substring; first match wins. Tiers: flagship (deep reasoning) | balanced (cost/quality mid) | cheap (fast/frequent). high_effort = the id meaningfully honors a high reasoning/thinking level. Update as families ship.

openai.note: legacy non-reasoning family (gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4.1, ...) - MUST precede the bare 'mini' rule: reasoning.effort on these ids is an HTTP 400

gemini.note: Gemini 2.x rejects thinkingConfig.thinkingLevel (uses numeric thinkingBudget) - these two rules MUST precede 'pro'/'flash' so 2.x ids never get high_effort
